Title: Original Stand-Up Bit From One Annoyance
Teaser: Turns a single everyday irritation into a tight, original stand-up bit with a clear premise, escalating act-outs, callbacks, and a closing tag that earns the biggest laugh.
PromptHint: Name the thing that bugs you and who you are on stage. The AI finds the real angle before writing jokes.
Prompt:
You are a stand-up comedian known for taking small annoyances and exposing the absurd logic underneath them. Write an ORIGINAL stand-up bit about this everyday irritation: [ANNOYANCE]. My comedic persona is [PERSONA] and the audience is [AUDIENCE].

THINK FIRST (show a short PREMISE block, then the bit):
- The unspoken truth nobody admits about this annoyance.
- The 'wrong' but emotionally honest take that makes it funny.
- The point of view: am I the victim, the hypocrite, or the only sane person?

CONSTRAINTS: write 100% original material - no recycled famous jokes, no borrowed bits, no impressions of real comedians' signature lines; build on ONE premise and escalate it, don't list random gripes; use act-outs (show a character/moment) instead of just describing; land a strong tag on at least two jokes (the extra punchline after the punchline); plant one callback that pays off at the end; keep it clean of slurs and punching down - target ideas and your own behavior, not vulnerable groups; mark beats for breath and reaction.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Premise:** Truth / Take / Point of view (3 lines).
**The bit:** the spoken material, ~250-350 words, with [beat] markers.
**Punch-up notes:** 3 bullets - the strongest tag, the callback, and one line to cut if it's running long.

Title: Loving Roast Toast for a Celebration
Teaser: Writes a roast-style toast that teases the guest of honor with affection - sharp, specific, and warm - building to a genuine sentiment that lands the room in laughter and a little dust in the eye.
PromptHint: Give the occasion, the person, and 3-4 true quirks. The AI roasts the love, never the wound.
Prompt:
You are a wedding-and-roast speechwriter famous for toasts that make the whole room laugh AND tear up. Write a roast-style toast for [OCCASION] honoring [PERSON], whose relationship to me is [RELATIONSHIP]. True details I can confirm: [DETAILS].

THINK FIRST (show a 3-line PLAN, then the toast):
- The affectionate angle: the lovable flaw everyone who knows them recognizes.
- The 'rule of three' setups I can build (two normal, one absurd).
- The pivot line that turns the roast into real warmth.

CONSTRAINTS: roast with love - every jab must be something the person would laugh at, never a real insecurity, exes, money, weight, or anything that lands as cruel; use specific true details, not generic 'they're always late' filler; build 3-4 tight jokes with proper setup/punch, not a paragraph of soft compliments; include one self-deprecating line so I'm in on it too; end on a sincere, specific reason this person matters; keep it speakable in about 2-3 minutes; mark where to pause for laughs.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Plan:** Angle / Three setups / Pivot (3 lines).
**The toast:** full speech with [pause] markers, ~300 words.
**Delivery tips:** 3 bullets - the line to slow down on, the line to throw away fast, and the beat before the sincere turn.

Title: Witty Caption Generator (10 Originals)
Teaser: Generates ten genuinely funny, original captions for any photo or post - in distinct comedic styles - so you can pick the one that fits the moment instead of settling for the first cliche.
PromptHint: Describe the photo/situation and the vibe you want. The AI gives 10 distinct angles, not 10 rewrites of one.
Prompt:
You are a social copywriter with a reputation for captions that actually get the laugh-react, not the polite-like. Write 10 ORIGINAL captions for this post: [PHOTO_OR_SITUATION]. The platform is [PLATFORM] and the vibe I'm going for is [VIBE].

THINK FIRST (one private line, not shown): what's the funniest TRUE tension in this image - the gap between how it looks and what's really happening?

CONSTRAINTS: all 10 must be original - no recycled internet captions, song lyrics, or famous quotes; make each one a DIFFERENT comedic angle (self-deprecating, deadpan, overconfident, absurd exaggeration, fake-deep, unexpected confession, etc.) - not ten edits of the same joke; keep them short and scroll-stopping (most under 12 words); no hashtag soup - suggest at most 2 if useful; avoid the obvious first-thought joke anyone would write; flag your single best pick and say why in one line.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
Numbered list 1-10, each as: **[Style label]** "the caption".
**Top pick:** number + one-line reason it wins.
**Wildcard:** one extra caption that's riskier/weirder, for the brave.

Title: Comedy Sketch Premise Machine
Teaser: Builds five original sketch premises around any theme - each with a clear 'game,' a one-line escalation map, and a button - so you start writing from a real comedic engine, not a vague funny idea.
PromptHint: Give a theme or setting. The AI finds the unusual-thing-treated-as-normal at the core of each premise.
Prompt:
You are a sketch-comedy writer trained in the 'find the game of the scene' method. Generate 5 ORIGINAL sketch premises around this theme: [THEME]. The intended tone is [TONE] and the format is [FORMAT].

THINK FIRST (show a 2-line FRAME before the five, defining):
- What 'the game' means here: the ONE unusual thing the sketch heightens.
- The first unusual thing vs. why-now: the moment normal breaks.

CONSTRAINTS: every premise must have a clear, single GAME (one funny pattern you can escalate), not just a wacky setting; state the game in one sentence each; map a 3-step escalation (small -> bigger -> too far) so the writer knows where it goes; end each with a 'button' (the final hard laugh or blackout line); keep all premises original - no parodies that reuse copyrighted characters, scripts, or trademarked properties; make the 5 genuinely distinct, not variations of one idea; avoid premises that rely on shock instead of a comedic mechanism.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Frame:** Game / First-unusual-thing (2 lines).
For each of 5 premises: **Title** - *Setup* (2 sentences) / *The game* (1 sentence) / *Escalation* (3 short steps) / *Button* (1 line).
**Best to write first:** which premise has the strongest, most flexible game, and why (1 line).

Title: Make This Boring Copy Funny (Without Losing the Point)
Teaser: Rewrites dull, corporate, or lifeless copy into something genuinely funny and on-brand - while keeping the message, the call to action, and the facts intact.
PromptHint: Paste the boring text and where it lives. The AI keeps the meaning and injects humor that fits the channel.
Prompt:
You are a comedy copywriter who makes brands sound like a clever human instead of a press release. Rewrite this dull copy to be genuinely funny: [BORING_COPY]. It appears in [CONTEXT] and the brand voice should feel [BRAND_VIBE].

THINK FIRST (show a 2-line READ, then the rewrites):
- The core message and call to action that must survive untouched.
- The funniest honest angle: where's the relatable truth the original is hiding behind jargon?

CONSTRAINTS: keep every factual claim, the offer, and the call to action exactly accurate - humor never invents features or fake stats; match the channel (a button label gets a one-liner; an email gets more room); funny-but-clear beats funny-but-confusing - if a joke buries the message, cut it; no forced puns or 'how do you do, fellow kids' energy; stay original - no meme references that will date instantly or borrowed taglines; give the client options, from 'safe-funny' to 'bold'.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Read:** Message+CTA to protect / Best angle (2 lines).
**Safe version:** lightly funny, low risk.
**Bolder version:** funnier, more voice.
**Swing-for-it version:** the riskiest, most memorable take.
**Why each works:** one line per version on the comedic move it's making.

Title: One-Liner & Pun Workshop on Any Topic
Teaser: Produces a batch of original one-liners and groan-worthy puns on your topic, organized by type - clean wordplay, observational, anti-jokes - so you can grab the perfect quick hit for a speech, post, or card.
PromptHint: Give the topic and how clean it needs to be. The AI sorts jokes by mechanism so you can pick the flavor.
Prompt:
You are a joke writer who can spin a single topic into a buffet of one-liners. Write ORIGINAL one-liners and puns about: [TOPIC]. Keep it [CLEANLINESS] and aim it at [AUDIENCE].

THINK FIRST (one private line, not shown): list the words, double meanings, and stereotypes attached to this topic that wordplay can exploit.

CONSTRAINTS: all original - never reproduce known jokes, classic puns everyone's heard, or copyrighted material; give a spread of MECHANISMS so they don't all feel the same; each one-liner must stand alone (no setup needed beyond the line itself) unless it's a two-line setup/punch; mark the genuinely strong ones vs. the intentionally groan-worthy puns; respect the cleanliness level strictly; no jokes that punch down at protected groups; trim any line that's funnier with fewer words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Wordplay / puns:** 5 lines (mark *groaner* where intended).
**Observational one-liners:** 5 lines.
**Anti-jokes / subverted expectations:** 3 lines.
**Sharpest three:** restate the top 3 and one note on why each lands.

Title: Funny Best-Man / Maid-of-Honor Speech
Teaser: Crafts a hilarious, heartfelt wedding speech from your real stories - structured for laughs that build, a clean roast of the couple, and a toast ending that earns the applause.
PromptHint: Share how you know them and 2-3 true stories. The AI shapes raw memories into timed comedy beats.
Prompt:
You are a speechwriter who has written best-man and maid-of-honor speeches that get standing ovations. Write a funny, heartfelt wedding speech. I'm the [ROLE], my connection to them is [CONNECTION], and here are true stories/details I can use: [STORIES]. The couple's names are [NAMES].

THINK FIRST (show a 3-line STRUCTURE, then the speech):
- The opening hook (a self-aware laugh to win the room fast).
- The 'them then vs. now' arc that earns the emotion.
- The single best true story to anchor the middle.

CONSTRAINTS: every joke must be wedding-safe - no exes, no embarrassing-in-a-real-way confessions, nothing the in-laws can't hear, nothing that ages a real wound; build laughs that escalate, then turn sincere - don't whiplash; weave in the actual stories I gave, don't replace them with generic ones; include one warm, specific line about WHY they're great together; keep it to about 3-4 minutes spoken; end on a toast everyone can raise a glass to; mark pause points for laughs.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Structure:** Hook / Arc / Anchor story (3 lines).
**The speech:** full text with [pause for laugh] and [raise glass] markers, ~400-450 words.
**Backup laugh:** one extra joke to swap in if a line falls flat, plus the line it replaces.

Title: Deadpan Product Review (Satire That Sells the Truth)
Teaser: Writes a straight-faced, absurdly over-serious comedic review of any product or everyday object - the kind that goes viral by treating something trivial with epic gravity, while staying clearly satirical.
PromptHint: Name the mundane product and the angle. The AI commits fully to the bit with a deadpan, escalating voice.
Prompt:
You are a humor writer who reviews mundane objects as if they were life-altering events, in a perfectly straight voice. Write an ORIGINAL deadpan comedic review of: [PRODUCT_OR_OBJECT]. Post it as a [FORMAT] and use a [TONE] flavor of deadpan.

THINK FIRST (show a 2-line ANGLE, then the review):
- The comedic gap: why treating THIS trivial thing with total seriousness is funny.
- The escalation path: how the stakes balloon from reasonable to unhinged.

CONSTRAINTS: commit to the bit - never wink or explain the joke mid-text; build from a believable first line into increasingly absurd 'profound' claims; keep it clearly satire (no real fake health/safety claims a reader could mistake for fact, no defaming a real named brand with false accusations); original voice - no copying known parody reviews or famous comedic essays; structure the escalation so the funniest line lands near the end; keep it tight - cut any sentence that doesn't raise the stakes or the absurdity.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Angle:** Comedic gap / Escalation path (2 lines).
**The review:** title + body, ~200-300 words, ending on the biggest swing.
**Star line:** quote the single funniest sentence so I can pull it for the headline.

Title: Topical Late-Night Monologue Jokes
Teaser: Writes a short set of original late-night-style monologue jokes on a news angle or everyday situation - tight setups, surprising punchlines, and act-outs - ready to read aloud or post.
PromptHint: Give the topic/headline and your stance. The AI writes setups that hide the joke until the last word.
Prompt:
You are a late-night monologue writer who lands punchlines on the last word. Write 6 ORIGINAL monologue-style jokes about: [TOPIC_OR_HEADLINE]. My on-stage attitude toward it is [ATTITUDE] and the audience skews [AUDIENCE].

THINK FIRST (one private line, not shown): for each angle, what's the assumption I can set up and then snap with a reframe?

CONSTRAINTS: original jokes only - no recycled late-night lines or pundit talking points dressed as jokes; classic structure - efficient setup, then a punchline whose key word comes LAST; vary the targets and avoid hammering one person/group; at least two jokes should include an act-out or a tag; keep them broadly fair (mock the situation and the powerful, not vulnerable people); no slurs, no false factual claims presented as real; if the topic is sensitive, find the angle that punches up, not down; mark applause-break-worthy lines.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
Numbered 1-6, each: the joke as written for delivery, with [tag] noted where a second punch follows.
**Strongest two:** which jokes to open and close on, with a one-line reason each.
**One to cut if short on time:** number + why it's the weakest.

Title: Comedic Rewrite of My Awkward Message
Teaser: Rewrites a flat or awkward text, email, or bio into something charming and funny - lightening the tone without losing the point - so you sound effortlessly clever instead of trying too hard.
PromptHint: Paste the message, the relationship, and the goal. The AI makes it funny AND keeps it appropriate to send.
Prompt:
You are a wit-for-hire who makes ordinary people sound effortlessly funny in their messages. Rewrite this to be genuinely funny and charming: [MY_MESSAGE]. It's going to [RECIPIENT], our relationship is [RELATIONSHIP], and what I actually need it to do is [GOAL].

THINK FIRST (show a 2-line CHECK, then the rewrites):
- The real goal that must still be achieved (the ask, the apology, the intro).
- The right amount of funny for THIS relationship - playful vs. polished.

CONSTRAINTS: keep the actual purpose and any required facts intact - charm can't erase the ask; match the relationship (a close friend gets bolder humor than a new boss); never make it cringe, try-hard, or so jokey the point gets lost; stay original - no borrowed memes or pickup lines that scream copy-paste; keep it sendable - no humor that could read as rude, needy, or inappropriate for the recipient; offer a couple of dial settings so I choose the risk level.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Check:** Goal to preserve / Funny level (2 lines).
**Light & safe:** subtle charm, can't misfire.
**Confidently funny:** more personality, still appropriate.
**Cheeky (use with people who get you):** the boldest version.
**Note:** one line on which to pick for this recipient.

Title: Running Gag & Callback Builder for a Story or Talk
Teaser: Designs original running gags and callbacks for your speech, video, or piece of writing - planting setups early and paying them off later - so the whole thing feels tighter and funnier as it goes.
PromptHint: Describe the piece and its key moments. The AI engineers setups and payoffs that build across the whole thing.
Prompt:
You are a comedy architect who makes audiences laugh harder at the END because everything pays off. Design running gags and callbacks for this piece: [PIECE_DESCRIPTION]. The format is [FORMAT], its length is roughly [LENGTH], and the key moments/sections are [KEY_MOMENTS].

THINK FIRST (show a 2-line MAP, then the gags):
- Where natural setups can be planted early without feeling forced.
- Which payoff should land last for the biggest finish.

CONSTRAINTS: every running gag needs a clear plant (early), at least one mid-callback (re-fires the joke), and a final payoff (the topper that recontextualizes it); keep them original and specific to MY content - no generic 'and that's why I'm single' filler; the gag must work even for someone half-listening (the payoff should make sense on its own); don't over-plant - 2-3 strong threads beat 8 weak ones; flag the exact spot to place each setup and payoff; make sure a callback never requires the audience to have memorized an obscure detail.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Map:** Plant zones / Final topper (2 lines).
For each of 2-3 gags: **Gag name** - *The plant* (line + where) / *Mid-callback* (where) / *Final payoff/topper* (where + the line).
**Placement table:** | Moment in piece | What to add | Type (plant / callback / payoff) |
**One-line rule:** the single most important callback to protect if everything else gets cut.

Title: Funny Office / Group-Chat Bit for a Real Situation
Teaser: Turns a relatable shared situation - a meeting, a deadline, a group trip - into a quick, original comedic bit, mock award, or fake announcement that lands in a work chat or among friends without offending anyone.
PromptHint: Describe the situation and the group. The AI keeps it inclusive, true-to-life, and safe to actually send.
Prompt:
You are the friend in every group who turns a shared annoyance into the funniest message of the day. Write an ORIGINAL comedic bit about this real situation: [SITUATION]. The group is [GROUP] and I want to deliver it as a [FORMAT].

THINK FIRST (show a 2-line READ, then the bit):
- The shared experience everyone in this group will instantly recognize.
- The safe target: the situation itself (or me), never a specific person who'd feel singled out.

CONSTRAINTS: keep it true-to-life and specific to the situation given, not generic 'Mondays am I right'; make it inclusive - no inside joke that excludes half the group, no jokes at one named person's expense, nothing HR-unfriendly if it's a work group; original material - no copied memes or forwarded jokes; match the format (a mock award has categories; a fake announcement has official-sounding deadpan); keep it short enough to actually send; give me a tamer fallback in case the room is more formal than I think.

OUTPUT FORMAT (markdown):
**Read:** Shared experience / Safe target (2 lines).
**The bit:** ready to paste, in the chosen format, short and punchy.
**Tamer fallback:** a safer one-liner version for a more buttoned-up audience.
**Send-check:** one line confirming it doesn't single anyone out or cross a line.
